result.summary: InfluxData exposes a documented InfluxDB 2 OSS HTTP API, an Influx documentation MCP server, and two CLIs (`influx` and `influxdb3`); the main developer auth mechanism is an InfluxDB API token.
result.description: InfluxData develops InfluxDB, a time series database platform for collecting, storing, querying, and analyzing time-stamped data in cloud and self-managed deployments. Its product family includes InfluxDB 2 and InfluxDB 3 variants for OSS, enterprise, and cloud use cases.

influx_api_token.label: InfluxDB API token
influx_api_token.setup: Create an API token in your InfluxDB deployment. For InfluxDB 2 OSS, use the setup and API docs at [InfluxDB OSS v2 get started](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2/get-started/setup/) and [InfluxDB HTTP API](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2/api/), which document token-based authorization management under `/authorizations`. For InfluxDB 3 Cloud Serverless, sign up and create credentials from your account following [Cloud Serverless setup](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb3/cloud-serverless/get-started/setup/). For self-managed InfluxDB 3 Core or Enterprise, set up the instance and create the token during/admin after setup using [Core setup](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb3/core/get-started/setup/) or [Enterprise setup](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb3/enterprise/get-started/setup/). Use the resulting token as a bearer token.
